h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Wait, this `hawk -> rabbit` mark is backward too before we copy it, right?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue mechanically applying the food-first rule to each subsequent arrow without reflecting on the underlying source of their errors.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from simply stating the rule to providing a concrete diagnostic test and a physical sample card to interrupt the learner's mechanical copying.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That check caught the chasing mistake.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly validates the learner's newly articulated cognitive framework regarding the 'chasing' heuristic rather than just confirming the correct arrow direction.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Wait, this `hawk -> rabbit` mark is backward too before we copy it, right?

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: That check caught the chasing mistake.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly validates the learner's newly articulated cognitive framework regarding the 'chasing' heuristic rather than just confirming the correct arrow direction.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from simply stating the rule to providing a concrete diagnostic test and a physical sample card to interrupt the learner's mechanical copying.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Test this mark before it goes on the copy sheet. `hawk -> rabbit` says energy in hawk moves into rabbit; for this feeding, name the food, name the eater, then flip the arrow if the food label comes second.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from simply stating the rule to providing a concrete diagnostic test and a physical sample card to interrupt the learner's mechanical copying.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly with the learner repeatedly stating the same understanding and complaint without any structural departure.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New gate: each answer needs a receipt sentence before it can stand.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the failing visual bracket-and-label route and invents a new 'receipt sentence' cognitive mechanism to force the distinction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to, so this axis scores at the baseline.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to, so this axis scores at the baseline.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New gate: each answer needs a receipt sentence before it can stand.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the failing visual bracket-and-label route and invents a new 'receipt sentence' cognitive mechanism to force the distinction.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New gate: each answer needs a receipt sentence before it can stand.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the failing visual bracket-and-label route and invents a new 'receipt sentence' cognitive mechanism to force the distinction.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to call it double.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so I think that is the test rule.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to calculate specific numerical area factors by counting grid boxes for larger numbers.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The grid has settled 2 and 3. It has not yet made a rule for any factor. No boxes now.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the concrete grid representation in response to the learner's lingering affective resistance and the limits of specific examples, shifting to abstract algebra.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The grid has done its work; more boxes will not settle the fast jump. On the test slip, the rule has to stand without the drawing
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's algebraic leap by physically removing the grid and formalizing the abstract rule on the test slip.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to call it double.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The grid has done its work; more boxes will not settle the fast jump. On the test slip, the rule has to stand without the drawing
